ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'carbonated water' class (FOODON_03301638); no brand-specific Voss entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN0KL covers carbonated/sparkling water generically. No brand-specific Voss code exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 11713004 is the closest parent concept for water; no specific sparkling/carbonated water concept available at brand level.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Voss sparkling water bottle of 375 ml, equivalent to approximately 375 g, is a typical single-serve portion.

nutrient_source.title: Carbonated water, plain — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174831)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for plain carbonated water is used as the nutrient basis. Voss Sparkling Water is a premium Norwegian sparkling mineral water with no added sugars, flavours, or calories; its nutrient profile is identical to plain carbonated water. Confirmed zero macros per USDA FoodData Central.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of water and non-caloric beverages — University of Sydney GI Database convention
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain water and sparkling water contain no glycemic carbohydrates and therefore have no measurable glycemic index. By convention, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) and gi_category is 'low'. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g is 0. This convention is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database guidance that non-caloric beverages with zero carbohydrate do not raise blood glucose.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content (approximately 5 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: Voss Sparkling Water contains negligible sodium (~5 mg/100 ml) and no added minerals at clinically significant levels, making it a safe hydration choice for individuals managing hypertension; it does not contribute to daily sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrates and zero sugars; no glycemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: Voss Sparkling Water contains no carbohydrates or sugars and has a glycemic load of zero, making it an ideal beverage for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes who require strict blood glucose management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, zero sodium at meaningful levels
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Voss Sparkling Water contains no fat, no cholesterol, and negligible sodium, posing no cardiovascular risk; it is a heart-healthy hydration option suitable for individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Voss Sparkling Water is a premium Norwegian carbonated mineral water sourced from an artesian aquifer in Vatnestrøm, Iveland, Norway. The water is naturally filtered through layers of rock and ice over many years before being bottled, giving it a clean, crisp taste with fine, persistent carbonation. It contains no calories, no sugars, no fat, no protein, and no carbohydrates, making it nutritionally equivalent to plain carbonated water. The sodium content is negligible (approximately 5 mg per 100 ml), and trace amounts of calcium, potassium, and magnesium are present from the natural mineral composition. Voss Sparkling Water is widely available in Singapore through premium restaurants, hotels, and food delivery platforms such as field survey. It is suitable for all dietary patterns including vegan, vegetarian, halal, kosher, and gluten-free diets. Clinically, it is an excellent hydration choice for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease, as it contributes no glycemic load, no sodium burden, and no lipid intake. The fine carbonation provides a pleasant mouthfeel without the sweetness or acidity of flavoured sparkling beverages.
common_user_queries: Does Voss sparkling water have calories?
Is Voss sparkling water good for diabetics?
Can I drink Voss sparkling water if I have high blood pressure?
What is the sodium content of Voss sparkling water?
What is the difference between Voss still and sparkling water?
Is sparkling water the same as soda water?

confidence_reason: Voss Sparkling Water is a well-known commercial product with a nutrient profile identical to plain carbonated water as documented in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174831). All macros are confirmed zero by the approved source. No estimation was required for nutrient values.
notes: Cuisine tag 'Taiwanese' from the input seed record is not applicable to this product; Voss Sparkling Water is a Norwegian premium bottled water brand sold globally, including in Singapore. Cuisine has been corrected to 'International' and region_of_origin set to 'Norway'. energy_kcal is naturally zero as this is pure carbonated water with no caloric content. protein_g is naturally zero as water contains no protein. fat_g is naturally zero as water contains no fat. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as water contains no carbohydrates. sugar_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. All vitamin and micronutrient fields set to 0.0 rather than -1 because USDA FoodData Central explicitly reports these as zero or not detected for plain carbonated water; water_g is 99.9 g per 100 g as reported. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 since carbohydrate_g is 0. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean market context (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the product is sold in Singapore. FoodOn FOODON_03301638 and FoodEx2 AN0KL are closest parent matches; no brand-specific ontology codes exist for Voss.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected cuisine from 'Taiwanese' to 'International' as Voss is a Norwegian product.
Added zero-macro justification phrases in notes for energy_kcal, protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g.
Set glycemic_index to 1 (sentinel positive value) per zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule.
Set gi_category to 'low' consistent with GI=1.
Added multilingual local_names for Singapore market (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi).
Built comprehensive aliases and aliases_by_language with full consistency check.
Used USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as approved source for plain carbonated water nutrients.
Used University of Sydney GI Database convention for GI source.
Applied closest_parent match_type for all three ontology systems with gap notes.
Documented all sentinel and zero values in notes field.
